# X-Gerät (X-Verfahren: # Station coordinates: de.wikipedia X-Verfahren + Jones (1978) pp.163-171
# Elevations: EU-DEM 25m terrain + antenna bunker height (~20m for large)
# Cotentin X-Gerät site = plateau above Baie d'Écalgrain ~150m
#   (Jones: "on the Hague peninsula NW of Cherbourg")
#   Using 170m effective TX height (150m terrain + 20m bunker/mast)
# Audembert X-Gerät = Pas-de-Calais cross beam site near Calais
# Julianadorp X-Gerät = Den Helder transmitter (Jones p.165)
# Morlaix X-Gerät = Ploujean airfield area near Brest (added Jan 1941)
# Modulation: 2000 Hz +/- 50 Hz audio (Bauer p.10; Price p.44)
# Bombing altitude: 6000m standard (Battle of Britain; Bauer p.11)
# Equisignal at 320km = 91m (100 yd) per Jones p.163
# 1936 trial: 300m x 300m CEP at 350km range (Bauer p.11)
#          Bauer pp.10-16; Radio War 2 Ch.2; de.wikipedia X-Verfahren
# BIRMINGHAM RAID 26/27 October 1940 - "first published bomb signature"
# Jones Fig 4 shows 3-pencil pattern 140-180m east of predicted aim
# PLYMOUTH / SW ENGLAND - Morlaix stations added Jan 1941
